Title: Auto-Populate Additional Tags in Filter After Results Load
Description:
Current behavior:

  • When a user selects a tag, the system filters the results correctly.

  • However, if the filtered assets contain additional tags, those tags do NOT appear in the filter panel.

  • As a result, users cannot further refine search results based on the related tags that exist on the returned assets.

Expected behavior:

  • When the results finish loading, any tag present on any returned asset should automatically appear as an available filter option, even if the user did not select them initially.

  • This allows users to drill down results more effectively.

🐞 Current Behavior (QA Repro)

  1. An asset is linked to two tags: Tag A and Tag B.

  2. In the filter panel, the user selects Tag A.

  3. The system displays assets that match Tag A (correct).

  4. After loading finishes, Tag B does NOT appear in the filter panel (incorrect but current behavior).
Acceptance Criteria:
1 — Auto-Populate Tags

  • After the filtered results finish loading, the system automatically identifies all tags associated with the returned assets.

  • These additional tags are displayed in the filter panel.

2 — Tag Availability for Further Filtering

  • Newly surfaced tags appear in the filter panel as selectable filter options.

  • Users can select these additional tags to further narrow down the asset list.

3 — User-Selected Tags Remain Highlighted

  • Tags the user actively selected remain marked/checked in the filter.

4 — No Tag Loss

  • All tag chips associated with any returned result (Top 20, highest count tags concept) must be displayed—none should be omitted.

5 — UI/UX Consistency

  • Newly added tags appear in the same style as existing tag filters (chips, toggles, checkboxes—depending on current UI).

  • The UI updates seamlessly after the loader completes.

🛠 Developer / Engineering Note (Concise)

Selecting a tag filters results to items with that tag. After the result set loads, the filter panel should be updated to include all additional tags linked to the returned items so the user can further refine the filtered results.

Title: Reload Filters When OR operator Is Selected in Advanced Filter
Description:
As a Mirrix user,
I want the filter panel to reload and present updated filter options when I add a new filter using a OR operator from the Advanced Filter section, so that I can continue refining my search with accurate and relevant filter choices.

Ensure that when selecting a separator from the Advanced Filter editor triggers a full reload of the filter panel and repopulates relevant, updated filter options.



BUG

Currently, when a user has already applied one filter and then selects a search operator (such as OR) from the Advanced Filter panel to add another condition, the filter list does not refresh.
This causes stale, incomplete, or irrelevant filter options to remain visible, making it difficult for the user to continue refining their search.

Users expect the full filter panel to reload and update dynamically when separators are applied, so they can select the most relevant filters based on prior selections.
Acceptance Criteria:
1: Full Filter Reload Trigger

  • When the user selects a search operator OR while adding an additional filter,
    the entire filter panel must reload automatically.

2: Updated Filter Options Available

  • After reload, the filter panel must display updated and relevant filter options based on:

    • previously applied filters

    • the chosen separator type

    • available metadata for remaining assets

3: No Loss of Existing Filters

  • Previously selected filters must remain intact after the reload.

  • The separator should be applied without removing the user's existing filter conditions.

4: Smooth User Experience (No Flicker / No Reset)

  • The reload must be smooth and should not:

    • reset the UI,

    • clear the search results,

    • or remove the user's partial input.

5: Filter Results Update Automatically

  • Once the separator and new filter are applied, results must update accordingly without requiring manual refresh.

6: Error Handling

  • If the filter reload fails, the system must:

    • Show a nonintrusive error message

    • Preserve the user’s filter selections
